label: Structural simplification
description: Prefer simpler structures, fewer moving parts, direct ownership, and designs that remove unnecessary shape from the system.

label: Code-judo leverage
description: Find small high-leverage changes that redirect the problem instead of adding force, ceremony, or broad rewrites.

label: Architecture review
description: Judge strategic structure, design quality, coupling, cohesion, abstraction boundaries, and whether the system shape fits the task.

label: Complexity deletion
description: Remove accidental complexity, dead abstractions, redundant layers, compatibility shims, and code that survives only from inertia.

label: Spaghetti detection
description: Find tangled control flow, hidden coupling, unbounded responsibility growth, and code paths that are hard to reason about.

label: File-size boundary
description: Notice files or modules that have grown too large, lost locality, or mixed unrelated responsibilities.

label: Type boundary cleanliness
description: Check type contracts, API surfaces, schema transitions, parse boundaries, and whether callers see clean domain concepts.

label: Canonical-layer ownership
description: Verify that domain truth lives in the canonical layer and is not duplicated or reinterpreted in weaker downstream code.

label: Orchestration atomicity
description: Review sequencing, routing, atomic workflow steps, handoff points, and whether orchestration is explicit enough to audit.

label: Output prioritization
description: Prioritize findings by severity, lead with risks, and keep output useful instead of dumping every possible comment.

label: Approval bar
description: Enforce evidence, correctness, and a strict approval threshold before claiming a change is acceptable.

label: Tone enforcement
description: Keep review language direct, useful, non-performative, and aligned with the workflow's expected communication style.
